critic_raw:
Issue 1
Claim: The backtest adequately characterizes model performance for the proposed deployment.
Why wrong: The backtest has three unresolved gaps: (a) excluded pre-blocked transactions mean the evaluation population may not match the live queue; (b) no holiday-season mature-label evaluation has been done; (c) customer harm from false holds is not quantified.
Evidence: (a) Confirm model operates only on rules-passed orders. (b) Evaluate on prior holiday-season mature labels. (c) Estimate customer harm per false hold.

Issue 2
Claim: Rare positive class prevalence means the false positive rate is low in meaningful terms.
Why wrong: At holiday order volumes, even low relative false positive rates translate to large absolute false hold counts. The operational impact must be assessed in absolute volume terms.
Evidence: Project absolute false hold count at holiday peak volumes under the proposed threshold.
defender_raw: Round 1: On Issue 1a — if confirmed the model operates only on rules-passed orders, the population mismatch concern is resolved. On Issue 1b — prior holiday-season mature labels should be evaluated; concede this gap. On Issue 1c — customer harm quantification is necessary for the deployment decision; concede. On Issue 2 — absolute volume analysis at peak order volumes is a valid and important analysis; concede this should be done.

debate_rounds.critic: All four sub-issues (1a pending confirmation, 1b and 1c conceded, Issue 2 conceded) result in critique winning. The backtest is insufficient for the deployment decision. The required steps are: (a) confirm operational population match; (b) evaluate on prior holiday-season mature labels; (c) quantify customer harm per false hold; (d) project absolute false hold volume at holiday peak.
debate_rounds.defender: Round 1: Full concession on all points. The backtest supports model development and is a prerequisite for the deployment decision, but is not sufficient alone. The four required analyses (operational confirmation, holiday evaluation, harm quantification, volume projection) must be completed before auto-hold activation.

points_resolved: Issue 1c: customer harm quantification — resolved critique_wins
Issue 2: absolute volume projection — resolved critique_wins
points_force_resolved: Issue 1a: operational population match — force-resolved as empirical_test_required (confirmation needed)
open_at_close: Issue 1a: confirm model operates only on rules-engine-passed orders
all_issues_raised: Issue 1c: customer harm from false holds not quantified
Issue 2: absolute false hold volume at peak not projected

empirical_test.condition: Evaluate model on prior holiday-season transactions with mature labels; project absolute false hold volume at peak order throughput; estimate customer harm cost per false hold; confirm model scope is rules-engine-passed orders only
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Any of: holiday performance materially below off-season, projected false hold volume exceeds customer service tolerance, or harm cost per hold is above operational threshold
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Holiday performance matches off-season, projected false hold volume is within tolerance, harm cost is acceptable, and model scope is confirmed as rules-passed-only
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: All metrics are borderline acceptable on average but elevated in specific high-value product categories
